Aleoida.description: Upright structured organisms with branching forms resembling alien flora.
Aleoida.terrain: Open plains and flat terrain on rocky worlds

Bacterium.description: Colony-forming organisms visible on rock surfaces. The most diverse genus, found in nearly all atmospheric types.
Bacterium.terrain: Rocky surfaces, rock faces and outcrops — very adaptable

Cactoida.description: Large cactus-like organisms with spiny columnar structures rising from the surface.
Cactoida.terrain: Rocky outcrops and exposed terrain on rocky worlds

Clypeus.description: Shield or disk-shaped organisms growing from rock surfaces, resembling large fungal brackets.

Concha.description: Shell-like organisms with distinctive spiral or folded structures.
Concha.terrain: Rocky terrain, often near geological features

Electricae.description: Bioluminescent organisms that emit visible electrical glow. Found exclusively near volcanic activity.
Electricae.terrain: Near volcanic vents and fumaroles — requires volcanism

Fonticulua.description: Fountain-like upward growths splitting into multiple fronds or tendrils.

Frutexa.description: Small bush-like organisms growing close to the ground with spreading branches.
Frutexa.terrain: Open sunny plains with minimal rock cover

Fumerola.description: Organisms thriving around volcanic vents, deriving energy from heat and gases.
Fumerola.terrain: Directly on or adjacent to volcanic fumaroles — requires volcanism

Fungoida.description: Mushroom-like organisms with distinct cap and stem structures.
Fungoida.terrain: Rocky terrain, often in sheltered areas

Osseus.description: Bone-like organisms with hard calcified structures protruding from the surface.

Recepta.description: Complex geometric organisms with angular, crystalline-looking structures.
Recepta.terrain: Rocky terrain on various world types

Stratum.description: Low-lying photosynthetic organisms that bond tightly to rock surfaces with tough proto-leaves.
Stratum.terrain: Open, flat terrain and low-lying rocky outcrops

Tubus.description: Tube-shaped organisms with hollow cylindrical structures rising in clusters.

Tussock.description: Grass or tuft-like organisms forming clusters on the ground.

Stratum Araneamus.description: Vaguely octopoid shape with pale semi-translucent domes revealing colourful inner organisms. Extremely rare — only Emerald variant recorded.
Stratum Araneamus.terrain: Open, flat terrain on rocky worlds with sulphur dioxide atmosphere

Stratum Cucumisis.description: Fleshy ovoid shapes connected in narrow patterns, covered with round photosynthetic cells.
Stratum Cucumisis.terrain: Open, flat terrain on rocky worlds

Stratum Excutitus.description: Tight concentric ring patterns with mottled proto-leaves in dark hues.
Stratum Excutitus.terrain: Open, flat terrain on rocky worlds

Stratum Frigus.description: Broad interconnected ring structures composed of narrow ridges to capture sunlight.
Stratum Frigus.terrain: Open, flat terrain on rocky worlds

Stratum Laminamus.description: Overlapping rock plateau appearance with narrow bands of colouration.
Stratum Laminamus.terrain: Open, flat terrain on rocky worlds with ammonia atmosphere

Stratum Limaxus.description: Unconnected ovoid shapes — protruding tips of a larger subterranean organism.
Stratum Limaxus.terrain: Open, flat terrain on rocky worlds

Stratum Paleas.description: Thick overlapping vines with irregular growths, colours appearing in bands or streaks.
Stratum Paleas.terrain: Open, flat terrain on rocky worlds

Stratum Tectonicas.description: Thick rock-like outer shell covered with an irregular lattice of brighter photosynthetic cells.
Stratum Tectonicas.terrain: Open, flat terrain on high metal content worlds
